reasoning: The Lean statement matches the informal claim component-by-component. `[NormalSpace X]` captures "X is normal"; `IsClosed A`, `IsClosed B`, `Disjoint A B` captures "disjoint closed sets"; the existence of open sets U, V with A ⊆ U and B ⊆ V captures "neighborhoods" (Munkres' convention treats neighborhoods as open sets containing the set, and even under the looser convention this is equivalent by passing to interiors); and `closure U ∩ closure V = ∅` captures "whose closures are disjoint". No hypotheses are added or dropped, and the quantifier structure (∀ disjoint closed pairs, ∃ neighborhoods) is preserved.
